methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Rivet Industries is a seed-stage company in the Defense & Security sector. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed). This reflects a broader landscape of eight private and two public Defense & Security companies used for market context.

The peer-median method is applicable and yields a median valuation of $225M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M across the four comparables. This $225M median serves as the primary triangulation point given the single applicable method in this analysis.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $191M (low), $225M (median), and $259M (high), derived from the peer-median result with a reasonable confidence band applied. Key uncertainties center on the composition of the peer set—three of four comparables are at Series A, one stage ahead of Rivet, which may bias the estimate upward relative to typical seed valuations. The analysis would benefit from additional seed-stage comparables and visibility into revenue, traction, or capital efficiency metrics that could refine the range. The wide peer dispersion ($60M–$500M) itself signals material variance in how the market values Defense & Security companies at this stage.
